question: Why does Matthew highlight ‘God with us’ at Jesus’ birth and then end with ‘I am with you always’—and how does that inclusio function as a theological claim about Jesus’ identity?

choices.A: Matthew frames Jesus’ whole mission as ‘God-with-us’ from start to finish, implying an enduring divine presence in Jesus’ person and authority.
choices.B: It is a narrative coincidence with no theological intent.
choices.C: It proves Jesus is only an angelic messenger.
choices.D: It shows Matthew contradicts the OT idea of God’s presence.

teaching.title: Matthew’s ‘God-with-us’ bookends
teaching.body: Matthew signals ‘Immanuel’ (God with us) at the beginning and echoes the same presence-theme in the Great Commission. As an inclusio (a literary framing device), it pushes the reader toward a theological conclusion: Jesus embodies and mediates God’s ongoing presence with his people, not merely as a prophet delivering messages.
